title: Cross-platform test matrix execution

context.goal: Manual smoke test before tagging release. Catches platform regressions cfg gating cannot.

linux_regression: Fresh `npm install && ./scripts/build-whisper.sh && npm run tauri dev`
Press Ctrl+Shift+D in Firefox URL bar, speak 'hello world', press again, text appears
Press Ctrl+Shift+D in VS Code editor, speak Spanish, text appears
Cancel mid-record with Ctrl+Shift+X — no paste happens
Settings hotkey Ctrl+Shift+, opens main window
Tray icon menu Quit exits cleanly
windows_smoke: Run scripts/build-whisper.ps1 — verify whisper-cli.exe in src-tauri/binaries
npm install ; npm run tauri dev — app starts, settings window appears on first run
Download tiny model from settings, set as active
Focus Notepad, press Ctrl+Shift+D, say 'hello world', press again — text 'hello world' appears in Notepad
Test unicode: say Spanish phrase with ñ — characters render correctly
Test cancel: start record, press Ctrl+Shift+X — no paste, overlay closes
npm run tauri build — NSIS installer produced under src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/
windows_install_test: Run produced .exe installer on a clean Win11 VM (no Rust/Node)
Autostart toggle creates Run key entry: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\dictate

failure_logging.required: On any failure, capture: console output (stderr from `npm run tauri dev`), settings.json, screenshot, exact reproduction steps. File as bug ticket before proceeding to T11.
